intent: We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture. You should apply Max impl_files per PR: 8–10. Beyond that, split into multiple PRs. Max acceptance criteria: 8–10. More than that is a scope problem.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS two-process AI coding agent comprising a Swift/SwiftUI shell (UI, Touch ID, Keychain, XPC) and a Python backend (consensus engine, build pipeline, GitHub integration, document store, multi-agent coordination, holistic review, TRD workflow, recovery/state management, CI pipeline, self-healing). The system is fully specified across 16 TRDs (TRD-1 through TRD-17) plus supporting documents. This scope encompasses all subsystems: the macOS application shell, consensus engine with dual-LLM parallel generation, confidence-gated build pipeline with pr_type routing, multi-agent build ledger, GitHub integration layer, holistic code review, TRD development workflow (FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT modes), UI/UX design system with health dashboard, Mac CI runner infrastructure, document store with FAISS vector retrieval, security threat model controls, backend runtime startup/shutdown, recovery and state management with build memory and build rules, code quality CI pipeline, and self-healing observability loop. Gaps in TRD-18 (Crafted Connect), Figma pipeline internals, EmailNotifier, WebhookReceiver, and ConsensusEngine.generate_single() will be resolved via the PRD process using best practices during build execution.

Clarifications:
  - Use the PRD process to resolve any issues using best practices.
  - Use the PRD process to resolve any issues using best practices.
  - Use the PRD process to resolve any issues using best practices.
  - Use the PRD process to resolve any issues using best practices.
  - Use the PRD process to resolve any issues using best practices.

Operator on 'Given the massive scope (16+ TRDs, Swift + Python, ~60 sourc':
full scope in a single PRD planning cycle

Operator on 'For the five identified gaps (Crafted Connect SDK, Figma bac':
generate specification PRDs first

Operator on 'Should the initial build target a specific milestone (e.g., ':
complete the complete system. Select teh most elegant order to develop the product and the code

Operator on 'The current repo already has src/ files (agent.py, build_dir':
select the best solution

prd_plan.title: Foundational Architecture, Repository Alignment, and Build Strategy
Gap Specification Pack: Crafted Connect, Figma Pipeline Internals, EmailNotifier, WebhookReceiver, and ConsensusEngine.generate_single()
Security and Trust Boundary Implementation Framework
Backend Runtime Lifecycle: Startup, Handshake, Command Routing, Degraded-State, and Shutdown
Swift Shell Core: App Lifecycle, Authentication, Keychain, XPC Bridge, and Process Management
Document Store, FAISS Vector Retrieval, and Context Integrity Pipeline
Consensus Engine and Dual-Provider Orchestration
GitHub Integration Layer, Repository Bootstrap, and CI Status Services
Build Pipeline Orchestration: Scope-to-PR Execution Engine
Recovery, State Management, Build Memory, Build Rules, and Context Persistence
Code Quality Pipeline, Local Validation Gates, and GitHub Actions Workflow Generation
Multi-Agent Coordination, Build Ledger, and Team Knowledge Sharing
Holistic Code Review Workflow and Fix PR Generation
macOS UI Foundation: Build Stream, Navigation, Context Panel, and Native App Experience
TRD Development Workflow: FOUNDER, ENGINEER, and CONSULTANT Modes
Self-Healing Observability, Detection, Classification, and Autonomous Remediation Engine
Health Dashboard, Diagnosis UX, Remediation Stream, and In-App Issue Reporting
Figma-to-Build Design Ingestion and Sketch Interpretation Flow
Mac CI Runner Infrastructure, Swift Validation, Signing, Notarization, and Release Artifacts
End-to-End System Validation, EmailNotifier Implementation, Operational Runbook, and Release Readiness

1.title: Project scaffold — Python backend directory skeleton
1.summary: Creates the canonical directory structure for the Python backend as the foundational skeleton. Adds __init__.py files for all Python subpackages: crafted_agent/, crafted_agent/consensus/, crafted_agent/build/, crafted_agent/github/, crafted_agent/docstore/, crafted_agent/review/, crafted_agent/xpc/. No logic, no config, no docs — skeleton only.

2.title: Project scaffold — remaining Python subpackages and test directories
2.summary: Adds __init__.py files for the remaining Python subpackages: trd/, agents/, security/, observability/, ci/, recovery/, and the tests/ mirror structure (tests/, tests/unit/, tests/integration/). Completes the full Python package skeleton.

3.title: Project scaffold — Swift shell directories and integration test skeleton
3.summary: Creates .gitkeep files for Swift shell directories (Crafted/App/, Crafted/XPC/, Crafted/UI/, Crafted/Security/, Crafted/Sources/Services/, Crafted/Sources/Interfaces/, Crafted/Sources/DesignSystem/) and integration test directories. Pure directory scaffold, no code.

4.title: Standards documents — ARCHITECTURE, INTERFACES, CONVENTIONS
4.summary: Creates the three foundational standards documents that all subsequent PRDs reference. ARCHITECTURE.md defines the two-process model, process boundaries, and communication topology. INTERFACES.md defines cross-cutting interface contracts (XPC message protocol, Unix socket auth handshake, event bus topics, error envelope schema). CONVENTIONS.md codifies naming rules, complexity ceilings (max 10 cyclomatic per function), import ordering, and security-precedence rule.

5.title: Build strategy and cross-process interface standards
5.summary: Creates BUILD_STRATEGY.md defining the build pipeline strategy, dependency management, and artifact production rules. Creates CROSS_PROCESS_INTERFACE.md defining the authoritative Swift-to-Python process boundary, authenticated Unix socket contract, request/response envelopes, lifecycle expectations, and forbidden interactions.

6.title: Global constraints manifest — NON_NEGOTIABLES.md and constraints.json
6.summary: Codifies non-negotiable global constraints: no direct LLM calls from Swift process, no Keychain reads from Python process, all credentials via XPC bridge only, complexity ceiling of 10 cyclomatic per function, security controls take precedence over feature delivery, two-process model is inviolable, no network calls without timeout + retry, all generated code must be sandboxed. Includes machine-readable constraints.json for CI enforcement.

7.title: Enforcement policy document
7.summary: Extracts non-negotiable security and complexity rules into an explicit ENFORCEMENT_POLICY.md covering two-process isolation, credential boundaries, LLM access rules, precedence of security constraints, and how CI gates enforce these rules. Complements NON_NEGOTIABLES.md with actionable enforcement procedures.

8.title: Package structure standards — Python and Swift module layout
8.summary: Creates PYTHON_PACKAGE_STRUCTURE.md specifying canonical Python package layout, module boundaries, import rules, and naming conventions. Creates SWIFT_MODULE_STRUCTURE.md specifying canonical Swift/SwiftUI module layout, target boundaries, service ownership, and app-shell layering rules.

9.title: File-to-TRD ownership map — FILE_OWNERSHIP.json and schema
9.summary: Produces the definitive machine-readable file-to-TRD ownership map per FR-1.4. Every existing file is assigned to exactly one TRD with disposition (keep/refactor/replace), rationale, blocking PRD, and security_relevant flag. Files required by TRDs but not yet existing are recorded as disposition 'create'. Includes JSON schema for validation.

10.title: Source disposition registry for existing Python files
10.summary: Creates SRC_DISPOSITIONS.json — a dedicated machine-readable registry for keep, refactor, and replace decisions covering existing src/ files, linking each decision to its blocking PRD and TRD. Provides granular disposition tracking separate from the full ownership map.

11.title: Implementation roadmap and dependency graph
11.summary: Creates ROADMAP.md containing the program-level implementation plan: ordered list of all PRDs, their TRD mappings, inter-PRD dependencies as a DAG, critical path identification, and milestone definitions. Includes machine-readable roadmap.json and DEPENDENCY_GRAPH.md for visual reference. Master sequencing document for the entire build program.

12.title: Versioning alignment standard
12.summary: Creates VERSIONING.md defining authoritative version source rules and synchronization requirements across VERSION file, pyproject.toml metadata, Swift bundle metadata, and CI tags. Single source of truth is the VERSION file.

13.title: Python project configuration — pyproject.toml and VERSION
13.summary: Creates the single source of truth for Python project metadata: pyproject.toml with package name (crafted-agent), version read from VERSION file, Python >=3.11 requirement, dependency groups (core, dev, test), entry points, and build system config. Creates VERSION file with initial 0.1.0. Configures ruff, mypy, and pytest sections within pyproject.toml.

14.title: Makefile and bootstrap script
14.summary: Creates the developer bootstrap experience: Makefile with targets (setup, lint, typecheck, test, test-unit, test-integration, clean, version-check, validate-ownership, check-constraints) and scripts/bootstrap.sh that creates a venv, installs dependencies from pyproject.toml, and verifies Python version.

15.title: Cross-cutting types — base enums, dataclasses, and error envelope
15.summary: Defines the shared type system used by all Python subpackages: ProcessRole enum (SHELL, BACKEND), Disposition enum (KEEP, REFACTOR, REPLACE, CREATE), PRType enum, ConfidenceScore value object, ErrorEnvelope dataclass (code, message, details, timestamp, trace_id), and BuildStatus enum. These are the atoms every later module imports.

16.title: Security interfaces — credential request protocol and sandbox boundary types
16.summary: Defines security interface contracts that enforce process boundaries: CredentialRequest/CredentialResponse types, CredentialScope enum (GITHUB_TOKEN, LLM_API_KEY, SIGNING_CERT), SandboxPolicy dataclass (allowed_paths, network_policy, max_execution_time), SecurityViolation exception hierarchy. Imported by XPC, build, and consensus modules. No Keychain implementation — that lives in Swift shell.

17.title: XPC message protocol types — Python side
17.summary: Defines Python dataclasses and serialization for the Unix socket message protocol between Swift shell and Python backend per INTERFACES.md. Includes XPCMessage (header with message_type, correlation_id, timestamp; body as typed payload), XPCMessageType enum, and serialize/deserialize functions using msgpack with size-prefixed framing.

18.title: Configuration loader and environment management
18.summary: Implements the configuration system for all backend modules: loads settings from config.toml (with environment overrides), validates against a schema, provides typed access via frozen dataclass hierarchy (BackendConfig, ConsensusConfig, BuildConfig, GitHubConfig, SecurityConfig). Enforces that secrets are never in config files — they come only via XPC credential requests.

19.title: Structured logging and trace ID propagation
19.summary: Implements structured logging subsystem: JSON-formatted logs with mandatory fields (timestamp, level, module, trace_id, message). TraceContext class for correlation ID propagation across async boundaries. Log sanitizer that redacts secrets matching known patterns. Configurable log levels per module via config.

20.title: Event bus — in-process pub/sub for backend modules
20.summary: Implements internal event bus that decouples backend subsystems. AsyncEventBus with typed topic subscriptions, at-most-once delivery, backpressure via bounded queues, and dead-letter logging for failed handlers. Topics defined as enum (BUILD_STARTED, BUILD_COMPLETED, CONSENSUS_REACHED, REVIEW_COMPLETED, HEALTH_CHECK, ERROR_RAISED). Each handler receives an Event dataclass with topic, payload, timestamp, trace_id.

21.title: Tests for foundational modules — types, config, logging, events edge cases
21.summary: Comprehensive test suite validating the foundational layer: enum exhaustiveness checks, ErrorEnvelope formatting, config loading with missing/invalid/override scenarios, log sanitization of secrets, trace ID propagation across async tasks, event bus subscription/publish/backpressure/dead-letter behavior.

22.title: Abstract interfaces — base classes for pluggable subsystems
22.summary: Defines abstract base classes (ABCs) that enforce interface contracts: AbstractConsensusEngine, AbstractBuildPipeline, AbstractGitHubClient, AbstractDocumentStore, AbstractReviewEngine, AbstractRecoveryManager, AbstractHealthMonitor. Each ABC specifies method signatures, return types, and required error handling per INTERFACES.md.

23.title: Backend runtime — startup, shutdown, and dependency injection
23.summary: Implements the backend process entry point and lifecycle manager: BackendRuntime class that initializes config, logging, event bus, and Unix socket listener in correct order; registers signal handlers (SIGTERM, SIGINT) for graceful shutdown with configurable timeout; dependency injection registry so subsystems obtain shared services without circular imports. Main entry point at crafted_agent/__main__.py.

24.title: XPC Unix socket server — connection handling and auth
24.summary: Implements Python-side Unix socket server: asyncio-based server bound to a well-known socket path, connection authentication via shared HMAC secret (received as first message per protocol), connection lifecycle management (heartbeat, timeout, reconnect), message dispatch to registered handlers by XPCMessageType. Enforces single-client constraint.

25.title: Tests for XPC layer — protocol, server, and auth
25.summary: Integration tests for the full XPC communication stack: socket connection/disconnection, HMAC authentication success/rejection, message serialization round-trip through actual socket, heartbeat timeout behavior, concurrent message handling, malformed message rejection, reconnection after server restart. Uses pytest-asyncio with mock Swift client.

26.title: Versioning alignment — sync script and generated version module
26.summary: Implements version alignment across all artifacts: scripts/sync_version.py reads VERSION file and updates pyproject.toml version field, generates crafted_agent/_version.py with __version__, and validates Swift bundle version in Info.plist matches (or logs warning if plist not yet present). Makefile target 'version-check' runs this script.

27.title: FILE_OWNERSHIP.json validation script
27.summary: Creates scripts/validate_ownership.py that reads FILE_OWNERSHIP.json, validates against FILE_OWNERSHIP_SCHEMA.json, checks every repo file is accounted for, verifies all referenced TRDs exist, and confirms no file is assigned to multiple TRDs. Integrated as Makefile target 'validate-ownership'.

28.title: Constraints enforcement — CI check for non-negotiable rules
28.summary: Creates scripts/check_constraints.py that statically verifies non-negotiable constraints: scans Python files for cyclomatic complexity (radon) failing if any function exceeds 10, checks no Python file imports keychain/Keychain modules, checks no Swift file imports urllib/httpx/openai directly, verifies error envelope usage in public module functions. Integrated as Makefile target and CI job.

29.title: Tests for repository conformance and ownership artifacts
29.summary: Adds tests asserting FILE_OWNERSHIP.json is parseable and conforms to schema, canonical package directories exist, required standards files are present, interface artifacts exist, and version alignment is maintained across VERSION/pyproject/Swift bundle.

30.title: CI workflow — lint, typecheck, and test pipeline
30.summary: Creates GitHub Actions CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) that runs on every PR: Python 3.11+ matrix, ruff lint, mypy typecheck, pytest with coverage threshold (80%), version-check, validate-ownership, and check-constraints. Caches pip dependencies. Fails fast on security-critical test failures. Separate jobs for unit vs integration tests.

31.title: Compatibility shims for legacy Python entrypoints
31.summary: Introduces minimal compatibility wrappers for existing Python entrypoints so the repository can transition to the canonical package structure without breaking imports immediately. Shims emit deprecation warnings and delegate to new canonical locations.

32.title: Swift shell scaffold — app entry point and XPC client types
32.summary: Creates Swift shell foundation: CraftedApp.swift (SwiftUI app entry point), XPCClient.swift (protocol and stub for Unix socket communication with Python backend), XPCMessage.swift (Swift-side message types mirroring Python XPCMessage), KeychainManager.swift (protocol for Keychain access — Touch ID integration point). Stub implementations that compile but return mock data.

33.title: Swift canonical interface definitions
33.summary: Defines frontend-side Swift protocols and models representing process boundary messages, app services, and shell-owned responsibilities per CROSS_PROCESS_INTERFACE.md. ProcessMessage enum, AppServiceProtocols, and typed request/response models matching Python-side XPC types.

34.title: Swift XPC client — Unix socket connection and message framing
34.summary: Implements Swift-side Unix socket client connecting to Python backend: NWConnection-based transport, HMAC authentication handshake on connect, msgpack serialization matching Python protocol, automatic reconnection with exponential backoff, heartbeat ping/pong, and credential response handling. Actor-isolated for concurrency safety.

35.title: Swift Keychain and Touch ID integration
35.summary: Implements KeychainManagerImpl: stores and retrieves API keys/tokens from macOS Keychain, gates access behind Touch ID (LAContext biometric authentication), supports credential scopes matching Python CredentialScope enum, provides secure credential lifecycle (store, retrieve, delete, rotate). All access requires biometric or password fallback. Errors surface as typed KeychainError enum.

36.title: Swift bundle metadata alignment with versioning standard
36.summary: Updates Swift application bundle metadata (Info.plist) and project-level version declarations to use the approved versioning contract from VERSIONING.md. Ensures CFBundleShortVersionString and CFBundleVersion can be synchronized with the VERSION file.

37.title: Integration tests — Swift-Python XPC round-trip
37.summary: End-to-end integration tests verifying complete XPC communication path: Swift client connects to Python server, authenticates via HMAC, sends build command, receives status updates, requests credentials (Python asks, Swift responds from mock Keychain), and gracefully disconnects. Validates message format compatibility between Swift and Python serializers.

38.title: Gap resolution PRDs — specification documents for identified gaps
38.summary: Creates specification PRD documents for identified gaps: PRD-GAP-001 (Crafted Connect SDK — TRD-18), PRD-GAP-002 (Figma pipeline internals), PRD-GAP-003 (EmailNotifier specification), PRD-GAP-004 (WebhookReceiver specification), PRD-GAP-005 (ConsensusEngine.generate_single() specification). Each follows PRD template format with functional requirements, interface contracts, and acceptance criteria.
